title: Sessionized Funnel Drop-off by Channel

landmines.name: out-of-order-events
landmines.kills: Reading a session's outcome as the event with the latest timestamp misclassifies inverted sessions (purchase logged before checkout), undercounting purchases. Deepest stage must be MAX over funnel ranks, ignoring timestamp order. Kills naive/1_last_event_by_ts.sql.

landmines.name: duplicate-events
landmines.kills: Counting event rows per stage double-counts double-fired (duplicate) events; the metric is distinct sessions reaching a stage, computed at the session grain. Kills naive/2_count_event_rows.sql.

landmines.name: correlated-subquery-tle
landmines.kills: Computing each session's deepest stage with a correlated subquery over the ~2.2M-row events table is quadratic and exceeds the time limit; the single grouped pass in the reference is linear. TLE for naive/3_correlated_deepest_tle.sql.

landmines.name: anonymous-and-divzero
landmines.kills: Anonymous (NULL customer) sessions must fold into 'unknown', and a stage with zero sessions makes its downstream conversion undefined (NULL via NULLIF), not a division-by-zero error or a spurious 0.

notes: naive/3_correlated_deepest_tle.sql is the designated TLE-slow solution: it returns the correct answer on small fixtures and fails only by timing out at Black scale (~2.2M events). The two WA naives fail on correctness at sample scale. Deepest stage uses the funnel hierarchy, never event_ts, so out-of-order sessions are classified correctly.
